page.summary.title: Persistent Cart for Shopify: factual plain-text summary
page.summary.desc: Persistent Cart is a Shopify app that syncs a signed-in customer's cart across devices and browsers. A plain, factual summary of what it is and how it works.
page.summary.h1: Persistent Cart for Shopify, in plain language
page.summary.lede: Persistent Cart is a Shopify app that keeps a signed-in customer's shopping cart synced across the devices and browsers they use. This page summarizes the product in plain, factual sentences: what it is, the problem it solves, how it works, who it's for, how it's priced, and its track record. No marketing language.
page.summary.s1.b: Persistent Cart is a Shopify app that saves and syncs a logged-in customer's shopping cart across the devices and browsers they use. When a signed-in shopper adds items on their phone and later opens the same store on a laptop, their cart is already there. The app runs on the storefront through a Shopify theme app extension and updates the saved cart whenever the shopper changes it. It works only for identified, signed-in customers; guests are not tracked. It has been available on the Shopify App Store since 2016.
page.summary.s2.b: By default, a Shopify store does not keep a shopper's cart across devices and browsers. The cart is stored in a browser cookie that lasts about two weeks, not on the customer's account, so it does not follow them when they switch from phone to laptop or change browsers. Large retailers such as Amazon and Walmart keep a signed-in cart across devices, and shoppers have come to expect the same elsewhere. When a Shopify cart does not carry over, the shopper often rebuilds a smaller cart or does not return — and because no error or email is triggered, the merchant rarely sees the loss. Persistent Cart closes this gap for signed-in customers.
page.summary.s3.b:
By default, Persistent Cart merges a returning shopper's saved cart silently. When a signed-in customer logs in, their saved items are added to their current cart with no popup, email, or opt-in. An optional setting lets a merchant instead show a 'keep or combine' prompt, but that is not the default. The app syncs only the signed-in customer's cart, keyed to their customer ID and store, so carts never leak between shoppers or stores.

Installation is one click from the Shopify App Store, followed by enabling the app embed in the theme editor. No theme code is edited. In short: one click and one switch.
page.summary.s4.b: Persistent Cart is for Shopify merchants whose customers sign in and shop across more than one device. It is most valuable to stores where shoppers browse on a phone and buy on a desktop, where carts are large, or where order values are high — for example wholesale, B2B, and parts retailers. It works on any Shopify plan, from Basic through Shopify Plus, and handles large carts. Because sync applies only to signed-in customers, stores that encourage account login, or that already have a logged-in customer base, get the most from it.
page.summary.s5.b: Every feature is included on every plan. Persistent Cart does not gate features by tier; instead, the price matches the merchant's own Shopify subscription level. A Free Starter plan lets a merchant install and test every feature for up to ten cart syncs. Paid plans start at $4.99 per month and rise with the Shopify plan level, up to $99 per month for Shopify Plus stores, and each paid plan begins with a 30-day free trial. Billing is handled through Shopify, and a merchant can cancel anytime from the Shopify admin.
page.summary.s6.b: Persistent Cart has been on the Shopify App Store since 2016, which makes it the original Shopify cross-device cart app and the most-reviewed in its category. It holds a 4.8-star rating and is Built for Shopify — Shopify's designation for apps that meet its highest standards for performance, security, and merchant experience. It has served thousands of Shopify stores over that time, including hundreds on Shopify Plus. The app is maintained by a small, founder-led team that reads and replies to support messages directly.
